_readme: All page copy, images, and buttons live here. Edit this file (then rebuild, or just save while `npm run dev` runs) to change any text, add projects or experience entries, swap images, or edit buttons and links.
profile = top-left brand (photo, name, subtitle). Nav menu links are structural and stay in index.html.
Image paths point into /public (e.g. /me.png, /images/foo.jpg). Leave an image "" to show the placeholder.
Buttons: 'icon' may be email | github | linkedin (drawn as an SVG icon) or omitted for a plain text button. 'href' starting with http opens in a new tab.

hero.title: Instruments that
hero.lede: I design and characterize satellite instrumentation - from propulsion test stands to flight software and sensor calibration.

about.heading: From the bench to orbit.
paragraphs: I'm an aerospace engineer working on satellite instrumentation. I came up through hands-on maintenance, which is where I learned how real hardware actually fails - and now I build and characterize the instruments that fly.
My work lives at the seam between mechanical hardware and the signal chains that measure it: propulsion test stands, CubeSat flight software, and flight-instrument bring-up.

items.title: Bi-Propellant Engine Test Stand
items.body: Designed and instrumented a 500 lbf liquid engine test stand - feed system, load cells, and a real-time data pipeline capturing chamber pressure at 2 kHz.

items.title: CubeSat Attitude Control
items.body: Wrote flight software for a 3U CubeSat ADCS - reaction-wheel control, sun-pointing mode, and a Kalman filter fusing magnetometer and gyro data.

items.title: Satellite Instrument Bring-Up
items.body: Current work: bench characterization and calibration of flight instrumentation - signal chains, sensor integration, and test automation.
Building and characterizing flight instrumentation - sensor integration and calibration for spaceflight hardware.
Stood up engine test campaigns - feed systems, load cells, and high-rate data capture for hot-fire validation.
Hands-on maintenance and troubleshooting of complex hardware - the practical foundation behind the work.
Prototyped avionics and bench fixtures - first exposure to flight hardware bring-up and lab automation.
Propulsion, controls, and flight software - from engine test stands to CubeSat attitude determination.
